key_insight: Detonation-sprayed AlCuFe quasicrystalline coatings form a stable, self-strengthening oxide-resistant barrier at 450°C, with oxidation following parabolic kinetics while the microstructure evolves into harder multiphase approximants.
engineering_relevance: This coating offers a promising protective layer for TC4 titanium alloys in long-term high-temperature service, helping slow oxidation while maintaining surface mechanical integrity.
method: Fabricated AlCuFe quasicrystalline coatings by detonation spraying (DS)
Aged/oxidized coatings in air at 450°C for up to 250 h
Tracked oxidation kinetics, phase evolution, and microhardness over time
evidence_signal: The coating obeyed parabolic oxidation kinetics with kp = 1.83 × 10^-2 mg^2·cm^-4·h^-1 and the surface microhardness increased from 491 HV0.2 to 665.2 HV0.2 after 250 h.
watch_item: Performance depends on long-term phase stability and Al depletion, so coating consistency and service conditions must be controlled to avoid excessive QC phase loss.
paper_title: High-temperature oxidation kinetics and microstructural evolution of detonation-sprayed AlCuFe quasicrystalline coatings
